content: The intrinsic reward signals used by curiosity-driven RL methods like ICM and RND to drive exploration can be repurposed to dynamically adjust an agent’s working memory capacity, allocating larger memory resources only to high-uncertainty, novel states that trigger the strongest curiosity rewards, and pruning stale, low-uncertainty memories to avoid capacity bottlenecks during reasoning. This creates a curiosity-gated working memory system that adapts resource allocation to exploration demand.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
